zh.Leighton, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾麗頓 白 內斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Hulse於1361年出生在英國爾拉比時，他的父親Hugh Hulse19歲，母親Ellen Bruen16歲。他於1390年與Margery de Domville在英國爾奧可頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1415年6月6日在英國英格蘭爾拉比享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Hulse was born in 1361, in Raby, Cheshire, England, his father, Hugh Hulse, was 19 and his mother, Ellen Bruen, was 16. He married Margery de Domville in 1390, in Oxton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 6 June 1415, in Raby, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Hulse(이)가 1361년 영국 체실르 라비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Hulse(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen Bruen(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1390년 영국 체실르 스턴에서 Margery de Domville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1415년 6월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 라비에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Margery de Domville в 1390 г. в местности окстон, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери.
